category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Language Arts / Unit 1 - Oral Communication

name.text: [LAN1] In oral communication, ACTIVE LISTENING means:...

questiontext.text: In oral communication, ACTIVE LISTENING means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Waiting quietly until the other person stops talking so you can respond
Fully focusing on the speaker, understanding their message, and responding thoughtfully
Agreeing with everything the speaker says so they feel comfortable
Memorizing the exact words spoken so you can repeat them back accurately

name.text: [LAN1] A PRESENTATION should begin with:...

questiontext.text: A PRESENTATION should begin with:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: A summary of everything you will say so the audience knows what is coming
An apology for any mistakes you might make
An engaging hook that captures the audience's attention and establishes the topic
A definition of every key term you will use

name.text: [LAN1] When speaking in a formal presentation, your voice shou...

questiontext.text: When speaking in a formal presentation, your voice should:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Be as loud as possible so everyone can hear without effort
Be varied in pace, volume, and emphasis to maintain audience engagement
Stay at exactly the same pace and volume throughout for consistency
Be soft and gentle so you do not seem aggressive

name.text: [LAN1] Eye contact during a presentation is important because ...

questiontext.text: Eye contact during a presentation is important because it:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Intimidates the audience into paying attention
Shows confidence and helps you connect with your audience
Allows you to see if anyone is taking notes
Prevents you from forgetting what you planned to say

name.text: [LAN1] Constructive feedback on a classmate's presentation sho...

questiontext.text: Constructive feedback on a classmate's presentation should:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Focus only on what was done well to build their confidence
Focus only on weaknesses so they know what to improve
Be specific, evidence-based, and balanced — both strengths and areas for improvement
Be as brief as possible to save class time

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Language Arts / Unit 2 - Reading Comprehension

name.text: [LAN2] To make an INFERENCE while reading, a reader must:...

questiontext.text: To make an INFERENCE while reading, a reader must:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Copy the exact words from the text that answer the question
Use clues from the text combined with their own background knowledge to draw a conclusion
Look up unfamiliar words in a dictionary
Read the text a second time more carefully

name.text: [LAN2] The MAIN IDEA of a non-fiction text is:...

questiontext.text: The MAIN IDEA of a non-fiction text is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The first sentence of the first paragraph
The most interesting detail the author includes
The central point the author is making, supported by all the key details
The title of the text

name.text: [LAN2] When you SUMMARIZE a text, you:...

questiontext.text: When you SUMMARIZE a text, you:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Write down every detail the author included
Copy the most interesting sentences word for word
Restate the most important ideas in your own words, much more briefly than the original
Add your own opinion about whether the text was good or bad

name.text: [LAN2] Text FEATURES in a non-fiction book include:...

questiontext.text: Text FEATURES in a non-fiction book include:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The author's opinions and personal feelings
Headings, captions, diagrams, glossaries, and indexes that help organize information
The font size and number of pages
The summary on the back cover

name.text: [LAN2] Reading CRITICALLY means:...

questiontext.text: Reading CRITICALLY means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Reading very slowly and carefully to avoid missing any details
Only reading texts that agree with your existing beliefs
Evaluating the author's purpose, evidence, and perspective rather than accepting everything at face value
Using a highlighter to mark important passages

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Language Arts / Unit 3 - Writing

name.text: [LAN3] The WRITING PROCESS includes these stages in order:...

questiontext.text: The WRITING PROCESS includes these stages in order:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Drafting, editing, publishing, prewriting, revising
Prewriting, drafting, revising, editing, publishing
Publishing, drafting, revising, prewriting, editing
Editing, prewriting, drafting, publishing, revising

name.text: [LAN3] A strong paragraph begins with a TOPIC SENTENCE that:...

questiontext.text: A strong paragraph begins with a TOPIC SENTENCE that:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Introduces the next paragraph's main idea
Provides a specific detail or example to support a point
States the main idea of that specific paragraph
Summarizes everything the writer has said so far

name.text: [LAN3] REVISING a draft is different from EDITING because revi...

questiontext.text: REVISING a draft is different from EDITING because revising focuses on:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Fixing spelling, grammar, and punctuation errors
Adding more sentences to make the piece longer
Improving the content, structure, clarity, and ideas of the writing
Choosing a better font and layout for the final copy

name.text: [LAN3] When writing a PERSUASIVE paragraph, which element is m...

questiontext.text: When writing a PERSUASIVE paragraph, which element is most essential?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: A long introduction explaining the background of the topic
A clear claim supported by specific, relevant evidence and reasoning
Using the most dramatic and emotional language possible
Including as many facts and statistics as possible regardless of relevance

name.text: [LAN3] Using SPECIFIC DETAILS rather than vague language in wr...

questiontext.text: Using SPECIFIC DETAILS rather than vague language in writing:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Makes the writing harder to understand because there is more to process
Makes writing longer but does not improve quality
Creates a vivid, clear picture for the reader and makes the writing more convincing
Is only necessary for fiction writing — not for reports or essays

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Language Arts / Unit 4 - Media Literacy

name.text: [LAN4] The PURPOSE of most advertising is to:...

questiontext.text: The PURPOSE of most advertising is to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Provide unbiased information to help consumers make good decisions
Entertain audiences with creative content
Persuade consumers to purchase a product or service or adopt a particular view
Report news events accurately and impartially

name.text: [LAN4] When evaluating a website as a reliable source, you sho...

questiontext.text: When evaluating a website as a reliable source, you should check:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Whether the website has an attractive design and lots of images
The author's credentials, the date of publication, and whether sources are cited
How many people have visited the website
Whether the text is long and detailed

name.text: [LAN4] STEREOTYPE in media means:...

questiontext.text: STEREOTYPE in media means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: An accurate and fair representation of a specific individual
A new type of camera used in film production
An oversimplified, generalized representation of a group of people that ignores individual differences
A technique for adding captions to videos

name.text: [LAN4] DIGITAL CITIZENSHIP includes which of the following?...

questiontext.text: DIGITAL CITIZENSHIP includes which of the following?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Sharing any content you find interesting regardless of copyright or privacy
Only using the internet for school-related tasks
Using technology responsibly, ethically, and safely — respecting others' privacy and intellectual property
Avoiding all social media platforms at all times

name.text: [LAN4] The POINT OF VIEW expressed in a news article or media ...

questiontext.text: The POINT OF VIEW expressed in a news article or media text:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Is always completely neutral and objective
Reflects choices made by the creator about what to include, emphasize, and omit
Has no effect on how the audience understands the topic
Is only relevant in fictional stories, not in factual reporting

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Mathematics / Unit 1 - Number Sense — Whole Numbers

name.text: [MAT1] What is the VALUE of the digit 7 in the number 47,392?...

questiontext.text: What is the VALUE of the digit 7 in the number 47,392?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT1] Which number correctly rounds 4,651 to the nearest hund...

questiontext.text: Which number correctly rounds 4,651 to the nearest hundred?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT1] Estimate 389 + 512 by rounding each number to the neare...

questiontext.text: Estimate 389 + 512 by rounding each number to the nearest hundred:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT1] Which of the following correctly shows 5,000 + 400 + 60...

questiontext.text: Which of the following correctly shows 5,000 + 400 + 60 + 3?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT1] A school collected 3,247 items in September, 2,891 item...

questiontext.text: A school collected 3,247 items in September, 2,891 items in October, and 1,456 items in November. What is the total number of items collected?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Mathematics / Unit 2 - Fractions and Decimals

name.text: [MAT2] Which fraction is EQUIVALENT to 2/4?...

questiontext.text: Which fraction is EQUIVALENT to 2/4?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT2] Order these fractions from LEAST to GREATEST: 3/4, 1/2,...

questiontext.text: Order these fractions from LEAST to GREATEST: 3/4, 1/2, 7/8, 1/4

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT2] What decimal is equivalent to 3/4?...

questiontext.text: What decimal is equivalent to 3/4?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT2] Which decimal is GREATER: 0.6 or 0.59?...

questiontext.text: Which decimal is GREATER: 0.6 or 0.59?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 0.59, because it has more digits
0.6, because six tenths is greater than fifty-nine hundredths
They are equal
0.59, because 59 is greater than 6

name.text: [MAT2] A recipe uses 2/3 cup of sugar. If you make the recipe ...

questiontext.text: A recipe uses 2/3 cup of sugar. If you make the recipe THREE times, how much sugar do you need in total?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 6/3 cups (2 cups)
2 and 1/3 cups

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Mathematics / Unit 3 - Measurement

name.text: [MAT3] A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 5 cm. W...

questiontext.text: A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 5 cm. What is its PERIMETER?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT3] A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 5 cm. W...

questiontext.text: A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 5 cm. What is its AREA?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 13 sq cm
26 sq cm
40 sq cm
45 sq cm

name.text: [MAT3] Which unit would be MOST appropriate for measuring the ...

questiontext.text: Which unit would be MOST appropriate for measuring the length of a soccer field?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Millimetres (mm)
Centimetres (cm)
Metres (m)
Kilometres (km)

name.text: [MAT3] If you leave home at 9:45 AM and arrive at your destina...

questiontext.text: If you leave home at 9:45 AM and arrive at your destination at 11:20 AM, how long did the trip take?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 1 hour 35 minutes
1 hour 25 minutes
2 hours 35 minutes
1 hour 45 minutes

name.text: [MAT3] A container holds 2.5 litres of juice. If you pour out ...

questiontext.text: A container holds 2.5 litres of juice. If you pour out 750 mL, how many mL of juice remain?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Mathematics / Unit 4 - Data Management and Probability

name.text: [MAT4] In a bar graph, the HEIGHT of each bar represents:...

questiontext.text: In a bar graph, the HEIGHT of each bar represents:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The category label on the horizontal axis
The frequency or count for that category
The title of the graph
The source of the data

name.text: [MAT4] A bag contains 4 red marbles, 3 blue marbles, and 1 gre...

questiontext.text: A bag contains 4 red marbles, 3 blue marbles, and 1 green marble. What is the probability of randomly picking a BLUE marble?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT4] The MEDIAN of the data set 3, 7, 9, 12, 15 is:...

questiontext.text: The MEDIAN of the data set 3, 7, 9, 12, 15 is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

name.text: [MAT4] An event with a probability of 0 is described as:...

questiontext.text: An event with a probability of 0 is described as:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Certain to happen
Likely to happen
Unlikely but possible
Impossible

name.text: [MAT4] A survey asks 40 students about their favourite season....

questiontext.text: A survey asks 40 students about their favourite season. 15 chose summer, 10 chose autumn, 8 chose winter, and 7 chose spring. What fraction of students chose SUMMER?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Science / Unit 1 - Habitats and Communities

name.text: [SCI1] A HABITAT provides living things with:...

questiontext.text: A HABITAT provides living things with:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Only food and water
Shelter only
Food, water, shelter, space, and the right climate to survive and reproduce
A place to compete with other species

name.text: [SCI1] A FOOD WEB shows:...

questiontext.text: A FOOD WEB shows:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: All the organisms that compete for the same food source
All the feeding relationships and energy flows among organisms in an ecosystem
The physical features of a specific habitat
How organisms physically move through their environment

name.text: [SCI1] Which of the following is an example of ADAPTATION?...

questiontext.text: Which of the following is an example of ADAPTATION?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: A rabbit eating grass in a meadow
A bear having thick fur to survive cold winters
A bird building a nest in spring
Two species competing for the same food supply

name.text: [SCI1] When a species is described as INVASIVE, it means the s...

questiontext.text: When a species is described as INVASIVE, it means the species:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Is native to the ecosystem and well adapted to it
Was introduced to a new ecosystem where it causes harm by outcompeting native species
Is endangered and close to extinction
Has been successfully reintroduced to its original habitat

name.text: [SCI1] BIODIVERSITY in an ecosystem refers to:...

questiontext.text: BIODIVERSITY in an ecosystem refers to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The total number of individual organisms present
The variety of different species and types of living things in an ecosystem
The amount of food available for all organisms
The physical size and geographic area of the ecosystem

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Science / Unit 2 - Light and Sound

name.text: [SCI2] Light travels in:...

questiontext.text: Light travels in:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Curved paths around obstacles
Waves that require a medium such as air or water to travel
Straight lines called rays
Circular patterns outward from the source

name.text: [SCI2] When white light passes through a PRISM, it separates i...

questiontext.text: When white light passes through a PRISM, it separates into:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Black and white only
The colours of the visible spectrum: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet
Two beams of equal intensity
Random colours depending on the angle

name.text: [SCI2] Sound is produced when objects:...

questiontext.text: Sound is produced when objects:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Emit light energy
Absorb heat from the environment
Vibrate and create pressure waves through a medium
Reflect energy off a surface

name.text: [SCI2] Sound travels FASTEST through which medium?...

questiontext.text: Sound travels FASTEST through which medium?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Outer space (vacuum)
Air
Water
Solid materials such as steel

name.text: [SCI2] The PITCH of a sound is determined by its:...

questiontext.text: The PITCH of a sound is determined by its:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Amplitude — how large the vibration is
Frequency — how fast the object vibrates
Volume — how much energy the sound carries
Reflection — how many surfaces it bounces off

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Science / Unit 3 - Pulleys and Gears

name.text: [SCI3] A SIMPLE PULLEY system is useful because it:...

questiontext.text: A SIMPLE PULLEY system is useful because it:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Multiplies the force applied, making it easier to lift heavy loads
Always makes objects move faster
Stores energy for later use
Converts electrical energy into mechanical energy

name.text: [SCI3] When two GEARS of DIFFERENT SIZES are connected, the sm...

questiontext.text: When two GEARS of DIFFERENT SIZES are connected, the smaller gear will:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Turn at the same speed as the larger gear
Turn more slowly than the larger gear
Turn more quickly than the larger gear
Turn in the same direction as the larger gear

name.text: [SCI3] In a gear system, MECHANICAL ADVANTAGE means:...

questiontext.text: In a gear system, MECHANICAL ADVANTAGE means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The system can run without any energy input
Less force is needed to do the same amount of work
The machine stores energy for future use
The gear system produces more energy than is put in

name.text: [SCI3] A COMPOUND MACHINE combines:...

questiontext.text: A COMPOUND MACHINE combines:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Two sources of power such as electrical and mechanical
Two or more simple machines working together
Gears and pulleys only — no other simple machines
Natural and human-made materials

name.text: [SCI3] When you ride a bicycle and shift to a HIGHER gear, the...

questiontext.text: When you ride a bicycle and shift to a HIGHER gear, the bicycle:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Requires more pedal effort but moves farther with each pedal stroke
Requires less pedal effort and moves more slowly
Automatically adjusts to match your natural pedalling speed
Switches from mechanical to electrical power

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Science / Unit 4 - Rocks and Minerals

name.text: [SCI4] The THREE types of rock are:...

questiontext.text: The THREE types of rock are:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Organic, inorganic, and composite
Hard, soft, and medium
Igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic
Primary, secondary, and tertiary

name.text: [SCI4] IGNEOUS rocks form when:...

questiontext.text: IGNEOUS rocks form when:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Layers of sediment are compressed over time
Existing rocks are changed by heat and pressure deep in the earth
Molten magma or lava cools and solidifies
Organic materials are preserved under layers of sediment

name.text: [SCI4] The ROCK CYCLE describes:...

questiontext.text: The ROCK CYCLE describes:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: How rocks are classified by colour and texture
The continuous process by which rocks are formed, changed, and reformed over millions of years
How humans mine and process rocks for industrial use
The rate at which different rocks erode in different climates

name.text: [SCI4] HARDNESS of a mineral is measured using:...

questiontext.text: HARDNESS of a mineral is measured using:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The Richter scale
The Celsius scale
Mohs Hardness Scale — from 1 (softest) to 10 (hardest)
The Beaufort scale

name.text: [SCI4] Responsible MINERAL EXTRACTION includes which practice?...

questiontext.text: Responsible MINERAL EXTRACTION includes which practice?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Removing as much mineral as possible as quickly as possible to maximize profit
Restoring land after mining, minimizing habitat destruction, and managing mine waste carefully
Keeping all mining operations secret to prevent public objection
Using only surface mining because it is less expensive

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Social Studies / Unit 1 - Medieval Times

n.text: [SOC1] In medieval European feudal society, what was the role ...

questiontext.text: In medieval European feudal society, what was the role of KNIGHTS?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: They owned vast manorial estates and collected taxes from peasants
They provided military service to lords in exchange for grants of land
They ran the only schools and hospitals in medieval society
They farmed the land in exchange for protection from the nobility

n.text: [SOC1] The Islamic Golden Age scholar Ibn Sina is best known f...

questiontext.text: The Islamic Golden Age scholar Ibn Sina is best known for:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Developing algebra and systematic methods for solving equations
Compiling the Canon of Medicine — a comprehensive medical encyclopedia used in European universities for over 600 years
Calculating the Earth's radius with remarkable accuracy in the 11th century
Creating the most accurate world map of the medieval period

n.text: [SOC1] The House of Wisdom (Bayt al-Hikmah) in Baghdad was sig...

questiontext.text: The House of Wisdom (Bayt al-Hikmah) in Baghdad was significant because it:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Served as the military headquarters of the Abbasid Caliphate
Was the largest mosque ever built in the medieval world
Was a grand library and translation centre where scholars gathered to translate, study, and build upon texts from ancient Greece, Persia, and India
Was the first formal university in the world, established before any European institution

n.text: [SOC1] Islamic scholarship reached Europe primarily through:...

questiontext.text: Islamic scholarship reached Europe primarily through:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Military conquest of European territories by Muslim armies
Translation of Arabic texts into Latin in centres like Toledo and Sicily, and direct contact during the Crusades
Direct teaching in European universities from the beginning of the Golden Age
The Catholic Church's official adoption of Islamic scholarship

n.text: [SOC1] A key difference between the Islamic world and much of ...

questiontext.text: A key difference between the Islamic world and much of Europe during the 10th century was that Cordoba (Islamic Spain):

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Had the most powerful military force in the known world
Was the largest city in Western Europe with running water, paved streets, public libraries, and high literacy rates
Had abolished the class system and established full equality for all citizens
Was governed by a democratically elected council representing all people

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Social Studies / Unit 2 - Political Regions of Canada

n.text: [SOC2] Canada has how many provinces and how many territories?...

questiontext.text: Canada has how many provinces and how many territories?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Eight provinces and five territories
Ten provinces and three territories
Nine provinces and four territories
Twelve provinces and one territory

n.text: [SOC2] Which level of government is responsible for PUBLIC EDU...

questiontext.text: Which level of government is responsible for PUBLIC EDUCATION in Ontario?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Federal government in Ottawa
Municipal government of the city or town
Provincial government of Ontario
The school board alone, independent of any government level

n.text: [SOC2] Nunavut was created in 1999 primarily to:...

questiontext.text: Nunavut was created in 1999 primarily to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Reduce the size of the Northwest Territories for administrative efficiency
Create a new province with full constitutional powers
Reflect the political aspirations of the Inuit people who make up approximately 85% of its population
Establish a new military base in northern Canada

n.text: [SOC2] The Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms (1982) prot...

questiontext.text: The Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms (1982) protects:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Only Canadian-born citizens
Only permanent residents and citizens who have lived in Canada for five or more years
All people physically present in Canada, regardless of citizenship status
Only people who have taken an oath of citizenship

n.text: [SOC2] Municipal government is responsible for:...

questiontext.text: Municipal government is responsible for:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: National defence, immigration, and foreign policy
Healthcare, education, and natural resource management
Local roads, parks, garbage collection, and local libraries
Issuing passports and managing interprovincial trade

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Social Studies / Unit 3 - Physical Regions of Canada

n.text: [SOC3] The CANADIAN SHIELD is characterized by:...

questiontext.text: The CANADIAN SHIELD is characterized by:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Deep fertile soil ideal for large-scale grain farming
Dense coastal rainforests and mild oceanic climate
Thin rocky soil, abundant lakes and rivers, the boreal forest, and rich mineral deposits
Flat grasslands formed by ancient inland seas

n.text: [SOC3] The collapse of the Grand Banks cod fishery in 1992 tea...

questiontext.text: The collapse of the Grand Banks cod fishery in 1992 teaches us that:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Renewable resources will always recover quickly without human intervention
Government regulation always prevents resource overuse
Even renewable resources have ecological limits and can be permanently damaged by unsustainable harvesting
Ocean fishing is no longer an economically important industry in Canada

n.text: [SOC3] For most Indigenous nations, land is understood as:...

questiontext.text: For most Indigenous nations, land is understood as:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Property that can be bought, sold, and exploited for maximum economic profit
Belonging to the Crown and available for development with government approval
A living relative, a source of identity, and a sacred responsibility — not a commodity to be owned
Valuable only for the natural resources it contains

n.text: [SOC3] The Great Lakes-St. Lawrence Lowlands has the highest p...

questiontext.text: The Great Lakes-St. Lawrence Lowlands has the highest population density in Canada because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: It is the largest physical region in Canada by area
The federal government required settlers to live there
Its fertile soil, mild climate, and access to water transportation and trade routes attracted the most settlement
It has the most valuable mineral deposits in the country

n.text: [SOC3] A RENEWABLE resource is one that:...

questiontext.text: A RENEWABLE resource is one that:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Is found only in underground deposits and takes millions of years to form
Is manufactured in a factory using industrial processes
Can be replenished naturally over time if used at sustainable rates
Will never run out regardless of how quickly or carelessly it is used

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Social Studies / Unit 4 - Citizenship and Community

n.text: [SOC4] ACTIVE citizenship means:...

questiontext.text: ACTIVE citizenship means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Holding a Canadian passport and the legal right to vote
Physical fitness combined with community volunteering
Making choices and taking actions that contribute positively to your community, regardless of legal status
Attending all levels of government meetings regularly

n.text: [SOC4] Non-profit community organizations differ from governme...

questiontext.text: Non-profit community organizations differ from government services because they are:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Always larger and better funded than government services
Required to serve all Canadians equally without restriction
Funded through donations and grants, governed by volunteer boards, and often serving specific community needs
Run by elected officials accountable to all voters

n.text: [SOC4] An effective CIVIC ACTION PLAN should be:...

questiontext.text: An effective CIVIC ACTION PLAN should be:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: As broad as possible — aiming to solve everything at once
Specific, local, and measurable — addressing one defined problem with clear success indicators
Designed so it requires no adult support to be credible
Focused only on writing petitions to politicians

n.text: [SOC4] Government services like public schools and libraries a...

questiontext.text: Government services like public schools and libraries are funded by:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Donations from wealthy community members
Fundraising events organized by community groups
Taxes collected from residents and businesses
Fees paid only by those who use the service

n.text: [SOC4] Truth and Reconciliation in Canada is connected to land...

questiontext.text: Truth and Reconciliation in Canada is connected to land because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Indigenous peoples voluntarily traded their lands for access to European goods
Land dispossession through colonization is a central historical harm that active reconciliation must address
All land in Canada was equally shared between Indigenous peoples and settlers from the beginning
The federal government has already fully resolved all land claims in Canada

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Health and Physical Education / Unit 1 - Active Living

n.text: [HEA1] Canada's 24-Hour Movement Guidelines recommend children...

questiontext.text: Canada's 24-Hour Movement Guidelines recommend children aged 5-17 get at least how many minutes of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity each day?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 30 minutes
45 minutes
60 minutes
90 minutes

n.text: [HEA1] Which of the following is an example of an INTERNAL mot...

questiontext.text: Which of the following is an example of an INTERNAL motivation for physical activity?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Wanting to win a trophy or competition
Wanting approval and praise from parents and coaches
Being required to exercise by a teacher or doctor
Enjoying the activity itself and appreciating what your body can do

n.text: [HEA1] The fitness component CARDIOVASCULAR ENDURANCE is best ...

questiontext.text: The fitness component CARDIOVASCULAR ENDURANCE is best measured by:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: How many push-ups you can complete before fatigue
How far you can stretch toward your toes
How well your heart and lungs sustain physical activity over an extended period
How much weight you can lift in a single maximum effort

n.text: [HEA1] A SMART fitness goal must be which of the following?...

questiontext.text: A SMART fitness goal must be which of the following?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Something achievable in a single day without prior practice
Focused entirely on appearance and body composition
Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound
The same goal as your most athletic classmate for motivation

n.text: [HEA1] Which of the following is a BARRIER to physical activit...

questiontext.text: Which of the following is a BARRIER to physical activity?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Having a safe park or recreational space nearby
Having a friend who enjoys the same physical activities as you
Social fear of being judged for athletic ability or physical appearance
Genuinely enjoying the physical activity you are doing

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Health and Physical Education / Unit 2 - Healthy Living

n.text: [HEA2] Canada's Food Guide (2019) recommends filling what FRAC...

questiontext.text: Canada's Food Guide (2019) recommends filling what FRACTION of your plate with vegetables and fruits at most meals?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: One quarter
One third
One half
Three quarters

n.text: [HEA2] Which of the following is a HEALTHY coping strategy for...

questiontext.text: Which of the following is a HEALTHY coping strategy for stress and difficult emotions?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Avoiding all social contact until the difficult feeling completely passes on its own
Eating large amounts of sugary food to create a quick mood boost
Talking to a trusted person, going for a walk, or engaging in creative expression
Pretending the problem does not exist until it eventually resolves itself

n.text: [HEA2] The FOUR dimensions of holistic health are:...

questiontext.text: The FOUR dimensions of holistic health are:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Physical, academic, recreational, and financial
Physical, mental, social, and spiritual
Nutritional, fitness, emotional, and intellectual
Medical, psychological, social, and cultural

n.text: [HEA2] Positive mental health includes all of the following EX...

questiontext.text: Positive mental health includes all of the following EXCEPT:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Feeling connected to other people
Having a sense of meaning and purpose in your daily life
Never experiencing negative emotions under any circumstances
Being able to manage challenges without being completely overwhelmed

n.text: [HEA2] If you are worried about a friend's safety, the BEST im...

questiontext.text: If you are worried about a friend's safety, the BEST immediate response is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Keep it completely secret because your friend trusted you and telling anyone would be a betrayal
Post about it on social media so more people can help
Tell a trusted adult immediately — this is the highest form of care, not betrayal
Wait several weeks to see if things improve on their own before taking any action

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Health and Physical Education / Unit 3 - Safety and Injury Prevention

n.text: [HEA3] The correct first aid for a MINOR CUT is:...

questiontext.text: The correct first aid for a MINOR CUT is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Apply butter or toothpaste to soothe and seal the wound immediately
Put ice directly on the wound to reduce bleeding
Apply gentle steady pressure with a clean cloth until bleeding slows, then clean with water and cover
Leave it completely uncovered so it can air-dry and heal on its own

n.text: [HEA3] Canada's emergency telephone number available 24 hours ...

questiontext.text: Canada's emergency telephone number available 24 hours free from any phone is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

n.text: [HEA3] Personal information that must NEVER be shared with onl...

questiontext.text: Personal information that must NEVER be shared with online strangers includes:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Your favourite book, movie, or television show
The sport or hobby you participate in at school
Your full name combined with your home address, school name, and daily schedule
Your general opinions about topics you find interesting

n.text: [HEA3] Young people's developing brains are more vulnerable to...

questiontext.text: Young people's developing brains are more vulnerable to the effects of substances because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: They weigh less, so substances affect them more strongly per kilogram
The brain continues developing until approximately age 25 and substances interfere with this critical development
Young people are less experienced at controlling how much they consume
There is no real biological difference from adults in how substances affect the brain

n.text: [HEA3] A VALUES-BASED refusal strategy for substance pressure ...

questiontext.text: A VALUES-BASED refusal strategy for substance pressure works better long-term because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: It uses complex excuses that are difficult for peers to argue against
It relies on fear of getting caught, which is a consistently powerful deterrent
It is grounded in who you genuinely are and what you actually believe, making it honest and sustainable
It requires memorizing specific scripts that cover every possible social scenario

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Health and Physical Education / Unit 4 - Movement Competence

n.text: [HEA4] A LOCOMOTOR movement is one that:...

questiontext.text: A LOCOMOTOR movement is one that:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Keeps the body in a fixed location while changing shape or position
Always requires a partner or group to be performed correctly
Moves the body from one location to another through space
Must always be performed to music or a rhythmic beat

n.text: [HEA4] The five ELEMENTS OF MOVEMENT are:...

questiontext.text: The five ELEMENTS OF MOVEMENT are:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Speed, strength, skill, stamina, and style
Running, jumping, throwing, catching, and balancing
Body, space, time, energy, and relationships
Warm-up, activity, cool-down, reflection, and practice

n.text: [HEA4] FAIR PLAY in physical activity means:...

questiontext.text: FAIR PLAY in physical activity means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Winning by any available means to gain a competitive advantage
Only competing against opponents at exactly your same skill level
Following rules consistently, treating all participants with dignity, and accepting decisions graciously
Scoring as many points as possible before opponents recognize your strategy

n.text: [HEA4] In a COOPERATIVE physical challenge, the primary goal i...

questiontext.text: In a COOPERATIVE physical challenge, the primary goal is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Identifying which group member is the strongest and fastest
Beating the other group by the largest possible margin
The entire group achieving the shared objective together
Each individual finishing as quickly as possible independently

n.text: [HEA4] A SPECIFIC and useful self-evaluation of a movement seq...

questiontext.text: A SPECIFIC and useful self-evaluation of a movement sequence is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: I did my best and I am proud of my effort
My balance held well in the opening section but my transition into the jump was rushed — I need to slow that moment down
It was pretty good overall and I think the class enjoyed it
I need to practise more next time to do better

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / The Arts / Unit 1 - Visual Arts

n.text: [THE1] The element of art called VALUE refers to:...

questiontext.text: The element of art called VALUE refers to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: How much a finished artwork would sell for on the market
The type of material and medium used to create the work
The range from light to dark tones within an artwork
The physical texture that can actually be felt on the artwork's surface

n.text: [THE1] NEGATIVE SPACE in visual art refers to:...

questiontext.text: NEGATIVE SPACE in visual art refers to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Errors and mistakes in composition that should be avoided
The emotional feeling created when an artwork has a dark or sad mood
Empty areas around and between the main subjects — as important as the positive space
The unpainted back surface of a canvas or paper

n.text: [THE1] Islamic geometric art typically avoids representing liv...

questiontext.text: Islamic geometric art typically avoids representing living figures in religious contexts because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Muslim artists had not yet developed the technical skill for figure drawing at that time
The tradition channelled creative energy into mathematical patterns expressing divine unity and infinity (tawhid)
Living figure art was considered too expensive and time-consuming to produce
Islamic art had no contact with figurative art traditions from other cultures

n.text: [THE1] The FIRST stage of the four-step art criticism framewor...

questiontext.text: The FIRST stage of the four-step art criticism framework is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Evaluate — judge whether the artwork is good or bad based on your reaction
Interpret — decide what the artwork means or is trying to say
Describe — list what you literally see in the artwork without yet interpreting it
Analyse — examine how all the elements are organized and related

n.text: [THE1] Providing EFFECTIVE feedback on a classmate's artwork m...

questiontext.text: Providing EFFECTIVE feedback on a classmate's artwork means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Telling them you like it and that it looks great so they feel encouraged
Pointing to specific elements you observe with evidence from what you actually see in the work
Comparing their work to yours and explaining what you personally would have done differently
Focusing only on weaknesses and areas for improvement so they know exactly what to fix

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / The Arts / Unit 2 - Music

n.text: [THE2] TEMPO in music refers to:...

questiontext.text: TEMPO in music refers to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: How loud or soft the music is at any particular moment
The emotional quality or mood the music conveys to the listener
The speed of the music, measured in beats per minute
The specific combination of instruments playing in the ensemble

n.text: [THE2] In 4/4 time, a QUARTER NOTE receives:...

questiontext.text: In 4/4 time, a QUARTER NOTE receives:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 4 beats
2 beats
Half a beat
1 beat

n.text: [THE2] TIMBRE is the musical term that describes:...

questiontext.text: TIMBRE is the musical term that describes:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The speed or tempo of a piece of music
The key signature and tonal centre of the piece
The unique sound quality that distinguishes one voice or instrument from another
The relative loudness or softness of a musical performance

n.text: [THE2] A WHOLE NOTE in 4/4 time is held for:...

questiontext.text: A WHOLE NOTE in 4/4 time is held for:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 1 beat
2 beats
3 beats
4 beats

n.text: [THE2] Nasheed is best described as:...

questiontext.text: Nasheed is best described as:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The call to prayer performed at Islamic religious centres worldwide
Islamic vocal music using the human voice — often unaccompanied — to express devotion, praise, and reflection
A specific style of Islamic calligraphy developed in the Ottoman Empire
A traditional Arabic instrument used in classical compositions

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / The Arts / Unit 3 - Drama

n.text: [THE3] DRAMATIC TENSION in a performance is:...

questiontext.text: DRAMATIC TENSION in a performance is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The nervousness and anxiety that performers experience before going on stage
The conflict, suspense, or uncertainty that makes an audience want to know what happens next
The silence between two scenes that gives the audience time to reflect
The volume and intensity of the performers' voices during a climactic moment

n.text: [THE3] The YES-AND principle in improvisation means:...

questiontext.text: The YES-AND principle in improvisation means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Always agree with your scene partner regardless of whether what they say makes logical sense
Accept whatever your scene partner offers and build upon it rather than blocking or negating it
Begin every line of dialogue with the word yes to signal agreement to your partner
Create two separate alternating scenes — one yours and one your partner's

n.text: [THE3] In ENSEMBLE performance, the most important shared valu...

questiontext.text: In ENSEMBLE performance, the most important shared value is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Ensuring every performer receives exactly equal amounts of stage time and audience attention
Having the funniest and most creative performer lead every scene
Actively supporting your scene partners so the whole group performs at its best
Memorizing your own lines and movements perfectly before any rehearsal begins

n.text: [THE3] A TABLEAU is:...

questiontext.text: A TABLEAU is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: A type of formal script format used in professional theatre production
A specific and distinct style of vocal delivery used in classical theatre
A frozen still image created by performers to represent a moment, emotion, or idea
A painted scenic backdrop positioned behind the performing area

n.text: [THE3] The most important skill in improvisation is:...

questiontext.text: The most important skill in improvisation is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Being quick and funny with clever and surprising verbal responses
Planning your entire character journey before the scene begins
Genuinely listening to your scene partner — more important than being inventive or funny
Having the loudest and most clearly projecting voice in the group

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / The Arts / Unit 4 - Dance

n.text: [THE4] The ENERGY element of dance describes:...

questiontext.text: The ENERGY element of dance describes:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: How fast or slow the movement is performed
How many dancers are performing simultaneously in the piece
The quality or effort of movement — strong or light, sharp or smooth, bound or free
The specific music chosen to accompany the performance

n.text: [THE4] Dance serves which of the following cultural purposes?...

questiontext.text: Dance serves which of the following cultural purposes?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Entertainment and artistic expression only — never ceremonial or spiritual
Athletic competition and physical training only
Storytelling, cultural identity, ceremony, social celebration, and spiritual expression — often simultaneously
Physical exercise with no communicative or expressive purpose whatsoever

n.text: [THE4] CHOREOGRAPHY means:...

questiontext.text: CHOREOGRAPHY means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The music specifically selected and licensed for a dance performance
Designing and organizing a sequence of movements with artistic intention
The costumes and overall visual appearance of the performing dancers
The emotional response experienced by the audience during the performance

n.text: [THE4] The key difference between APPRECIATION and APPROPRIATI...

questiontext.text: The key difference between APPRECIATION and APPROPRIATION of another culture's dance is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: There is no meaningful difference — all cultural sharing and borrowing is equally valid
Appreciation means performing the dance publicly while appropriation means learning it privately
Appreciation approaches a tradition with genuine curiosity, learning, and respect; appropriation takes elements without understanding, permission, or respect for the source community
Appropriation is only ethically wrong when done for direct commercial profit

n.text: [THE4] When observing a classmate's dance performance, the mos...

questiontext.text: When observing a classmate's dance performance, the most respectful response is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Quietly talk with a neighbour about something unrelated during the performance
Laugh if any movement appears unusual or unexpected
Give your full and genuine attention; respond thoughtfully and specifically when feedback is invited
Record the performance on your phone without first asking the performer's permission

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / French / Unit 1 - Greetings and Introductions

n.text: [FRE1] Comment t'appelles-tu? means:...

questiontext.text: Comment t'appelles-tu? means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: How are you today?
Where do you come from?
What is your name?
How old are you?

n.text: [FRE1] Ma mere means:...

questiontext.text: Ma mere means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: My father
My brother
My grandmother
My mother

n.text: [FRE1] The number 15 in French is:...

questiontext.text: The number 15 in French is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Quatorze
Seize
Treize
Quinze

n.text: [FRE1] Bonjour is the appropriate greeting to use:...

questiontext.text: Bonjour is the appropriate greeting to use:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Only during the evening hours
Only with close personal friends
At any time during the day with anyone in formal or informal settings
Only in very formal and official situations

n.text: [FRE1] Mon frere s'appelle Ahmed means:...

questiontext.text: Mon frere s'appelle Ahmed means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: My brother is named Ahmed
My friend Ahmed has arrived
I am named Ahmed
Ahmed is my best friend

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / French / Unit 2 - School and Daily Routines

n.text: [FRE2] J'aime les mathematiques means:...

questiontext.text: J'aime les mathematiques means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Mathematics is very difficult for me
I do not like mathematics
I really like mathematics
Mathematics is my absolute favourite subject

n.text: [FRE2] Le crayon means:...

questiontext.text: Le crayon means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The eraser
The ruler
The textbook
The pencil

n.text: [FRE2] Quelle heure est-il? means:...

questiontext.text: Quelle heure est-il? means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: What day is it today?
What is your name please?
What time is it?
Where is the classroom located?

n.text: [FRE2] Je n'aime pas means:...

questiontext.text: Je n'aime pas means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: I really like a lot
I do not like
I absolutely love
I sometimes like

n.text: [FRE2] Which French sentence is grammatically correct?...

questiontext.text: Which French sentence is grammatically correct?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: J'aime la science
J'aime de la science
Je aime la science
J'aimes la science

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / French / Unit 3 - Food and Celebrations

n.text: [FRE3] Je voudrais de l'eau s'il vous plait means:...

questiontext.text: Je voudrais de l'eau s'il vous plait means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: I do not want any water thank you
Is there any water available here?
I would like some water please
Water is very healthy to drink

n.text: [FRE3] Du pain uses the partitive article DU because pain (bre...

questiontext.text: Du pain uses the partitive article DU because pain (bread) is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: A feminine noun
A plural noun
A masculine singular noun
A verb not a noun

n.text: [FRE3] Joyeux anniversaire means:...

questiontext.text: Joyeux anniversaire means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Happy New Year
Warmest congratulations
Happy Birthday
Best of luck to you

n.text: [FRE3] La pomme means:...

questiontext.text: La pomme means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The orange
The banana
The bunch of grapes
The apple

n.text: [FRE3] To order food politely in French you say:...

questiontext.text: To order food politely in French you say:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Donne-moi du pain right now
Je voudrais du pain s'il vous plait
Pain maintenant please
Je veux du pain immediately

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / French / Unit 4 - My Community in French

n.text: [FRE4] La bibliotheque means:...

questiontext.text: La bibliotheque means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The neighbourhood park
The general hospital
The public library
The grocery supermarket

n.text: [FRE4] Je vais a l'ecole a pied means:...

questiontext.text: Je vais a l'ecole a pied means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: I travel to school by bus every day
I drive myself to school
I ride my bicycle to school
I walk to school on foot

n.text: [FRE4] En face de means:...

questiontext.text: En face de means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Located right next to
Hidden somewhere behind
Very far away from
Directly opposite or across from

n.text: [FRE4] The French sequence connectors d'abord, ensuite, finale...

questiontext.text: The French sequence connectors d'abord, ensuite, finalement are used to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Describe colours and physical appearances
Express personal preferences and opinions
Give directions or sequence events in a logical order
Ask questions about people and places

n.text: [FRE4] A cote de means:...

questiontext.text: A cote de means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Very far away from
Directly in front of
Completely hidden behind
Right next to or beside

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Islamic Studies / Unit 1 - Aqeedah — Knowing Allah

n.text: [ISL1] How many pillars of Iman (articles of Islamic faith) ar...

questiontext.text: How many pillars of Iman (articles of Islamic faith) are there?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

n.text: [ISL1] The angel responsible for bringing divine revelation to...

questiontext.text: The angel responsible for bringing divine revelation to the prophets was:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Mikail (Michael)
Israfil
Jibril (Gabriel)
Malik, the guardian of Hellfire

n.text: [ISL1] The four divinely revealed books are:...

questiontext.text: The four divinely revealed books are:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The Quran, the Torah, the Psalms, and the Hadith collections
The Quran, the Tawrat (Torah), the Zabur (Psalms), and the Injeel (Gospel)
The Quran, the Sunnah, the Ijma, and the Qiyas
The Quran, the Torah, the Bible, and the Dead Sea Scrolls

n.text: [ISL1] Knowing Allah's Name Al-Baseer (The All-Seeing) should ...

questiontext.text: Knowing Allah's Name Al-Baseer (The All-Seeing) should most directly affect a Muslim's behaviour by:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Motivating them to perform only visible public acts of worship for others to observe
Reminding them to behave with full integrity and honesty whether or not any human being is watching
Making them feel constantly and uncomfortably watched in a stressful way
Encouraging them to keep all their good deeds completely secret from everyone

n.text: [ISL1] The Islamic concept of Tadabbur means:...

questiontext.text: The Islamic concept of Tadabbur means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The obligation to perform the five daily prayers at their correct times
The annual pilgrimage to Makkah performed by Muslims who are able
Deep reflection and pondering over the Quran and the signs of Allah in creation
The practice of seeking knowledge from teachers and scholars of Islam

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Islamic Studies / Unit 2 - Fiqh — Daily Worship

n.text: [ISL2] The First Pillar of Islam is:...

questiontext.text: The First Pillar of Islam is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Salah — the five daily prayers
Zakat — the annual obligatory charity
The Shahadah — the testimony of faith: La ilaha illa Allah, Muhammadun Rasul Allah
Sawm — fasting during the month of Ramadan

n.text: [ISL2] Muslims are obligated to perform how many prayers each ...

questiontext.text: Muslims are obligated to perform how many prayers each day?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

n.text: [ISL2] Zakat is calculated at what percentage of eligible weal...

questiontext.text: Zakat is calculated at what percentage of eligible wealth held for one complete lunar year?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: 1 percent
2.5 percent
5 percent
10 percent

n.text: [ISL2] The primary purpose of Ramadan fasting according to the...

questiontext.text: The primary purpose of Ramadan fasting according to the Quran is:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: To lose weight and achieve better physical health outcomes
To experience the discomfort of hunger as a form of physical discipline
To attain taqwa — God-consciousness and ongoing awareness of one's accountability to Allah
To publicly demonstrate one's devotion and commitment to Islam

n.text: [ISL2] Khushu in salah refers to:...

questiontext.text: Khushu in salah refers to:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The physical posture and precise movements performed correctly in each rakat
The quality of genuine presence, humility, and awareness that gives salah its spiritual effectiveness
The recitation of the Quran in a beautiful melodic voice during prayer
Performing all five prayers at exactly their preferred earliest times

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Islamic Studies / Unit 3 - Seerah — Life of the Prophet

n.text: [ISL3] The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) was born in:...

questiontext.text: The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) was born in:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Madinah
Jerusalem
Makkah
Ta'if

n.text: [ISL3] Before receiving prophethood, Muhammad (peace be upon h...

questiontext.text: Before receiving prophethood, Muhammad (peace be upon him) was known as:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Al-Kareem (The Generous One)
Al-Amin (The Trustworthy One)
Al-Hakim (The Wise One)
Al-Qawi (The Strong One)

n.text: [ISL3] The first revelation was received by the Prophet:...

questiontext.text: The first revelation was received by the Prophet:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Inside the Ka'bah during a period of communal prayer
On Mount Arafat during the annual pilgrimage season
In the Cave of Hira on Mount Nur above the city of Makkah
In Madinah after the migration from Makkah

n.text: [ISL3] The Hijrah (migration from Makkah to Madinah in 622 CE)...

questiontext.text: The Hijrah (migration from Makkah to Madinah in 622 CE) is significant because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: It was the first military victory of the early Muslim community
It marked the founding of the first Muslim community with its own governance and social institutions
It was when the Prophet received the first revelation
It marked the conquest of Makkah and the end of persecution

n.text: [ISL3] Khadijah bint Khuwaylid is honoured in Islamic history ...

questiontext.text: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid is honoured in Islamic history as:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The mother of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him)
The first Muslim, a devoted supporter who comforted the Prophet after his first revelation, and his beloved wife
The first woman to memorize the complete Quran
The companion who narrated the most hadiths in Islamic scholarship

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Islamic Studies / Unit 4 - Akhlaq — Islamic Character

n.text: [ISL4] Sidq in Islamic character means:...

questiontext.text: Sidq in Islamic character means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Bravery and courage when facing physical difficulty or danger
Truthfulness in speech, action, AND heart — all three dimensions together
Generosity with both wealth and personal time for others
Patient perseverance and endurance through trials and hardship

n.text: [ISL4] Islamic Adab (etiquette) when entering someone's home i...

questiontext.text: Islamic Adab (etiquette) when entering someone's home includes:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Walking in directly without any waiting or announcement
Saying As-salamu alaykum and waiting for permission before entering
Going directly to sit in the most comfortable and prominent seat available
Knocking exactly three times regardless of any other consideration

n.text: [ISL4] Qana'a in Islamic character means:...

questiontext.text: Qana'a in Islamic character means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Ambitious desire to acquire more than your current situation
Jealousy and resentment toward those who possess more blessings
Genuine contentment and satisfaction with what Allah has given while still making lawful halal effort
Greediness and an insatiable desire for more wealth and possessions

n.text: [ISL4] Shukr of the LIMBS — the third level of complete gratit...

questiontext.text: Shukr of the LIMBS — the third level of complete gratitude — means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Simply saying Alhamdulillah with the tongue after receiving a blessing
Feeling gratitude sincerely in the heart without any outward expression
Using the blessing Allah gave in ways that please and honour the Giver — through worship and service to others
Counting and recording your blessings in a personal gratitude journal every night

n.text: [ISL4] Amanah (trustworthiness) in Islamic character is broade...

questiontext.text: Amanah (trustworthiness) in Islamic character is broader than simply not lying — it includes:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Only the obligation to tell the truth when directly asked a question
Returning what was entrusted to you, fulfilling responsibilities without supervision, and protecting confidential information
Being honest only with family members and close personal friends
Truthfulness only in formal business and financial transactions

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Arabic Language / Unit 1 - The Arabic Alphabet — Mastery

n.text: [ARA1] How many letters are in the Arabic alphabet?...

questiontext.text: How many letters are in the Arabic alphabet?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

n.text: [ARA1] Arabic letters change their shape based on:...

questiontext.text: Arabic letters change their shape based on:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The specific meaning of the word in which they appear
Whether the word is functioning as a noun, verb, or adjective
Their position within the word: initial, medial, final, or isolated
The regional dialect of Arabic being written

n.text: [ARA1] The fatha vowel mark placed above an Arabic letter prod...

questiontext.text: The fatha vowel mark placed above an Arabic letter produces:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: A long ee sound as heard in the word tree
A long oo sound as heard in the word moon
A short a sound similar to the vowel in apple
A silent pause with no vowel sound

n.text: [ARA1] Which pair of Arabic letters is distinguished ONLY by t...

questiontext.text: Which pair of Arabic letters is distinguished ONLY by the number and position of their dots?

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Ba and Jim
Ba and Ta
Alif and Waw
Mim and Nun

n.text: [ARA1] The word Bismillah means:...

questiontext.text: The word Bismillah means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Praise and thanks are for Allah
Glory and perfection be to Allah
In the name of Allah
Allah is the Greatest of all

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Arabic Language / Unit 2 - My World in Arabic

n.text: [ARA2] Al-ukht means:...

questiontext.text: Al-ukht means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Mother
Father
Sister
Grandmother

n.text: [ARA2] The Arabic preposition ala means:...

questiontext.text: The Arabic preposition ala means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Underneath or below
Hidden behind
Located inside or in
On top of or on

n.text: [ARA2] Adhabu ila al-madrasa means:...

questiontext.text: Adhabu ila al-madrasa means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: I sleep at school every day
I play happily at school
I go to school
I study hard at school

n.text: [ARA2] Uhibbu al-riyyada means:...

questiontext.text: Uhibbu al-riyyada means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Mathematics is very difficult for me
I do not like mathematics at all
I like mathematics
Mathematics is my teacher's favourite subject

n.text: [ARA2] Kam al-saa'a? means:...

questiontext.text: Kam al-saa'a? means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: What day of the week is it today?
What is your full name please?
What time is it right now?
Where exactly are you going?

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Arabic Language / Unit 3 - Quran Connection — Al-Fatihah

n.text: [ARA3] The word Rabb in Surah Al-Fatihah means:...

questiontext.text: The word Rabb in Surah Al-Fatihah means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The Most Merciful and Compassionate
The Sovereign Master and King
The Lord, Sustainer, and Educator of all
The Perfect Guide and Teacher

n.text: [ARA3] Al-sirat al-mustaqim means:...

questiontext.text: Al-sirat al-mustaqim means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: The Day of Judgment and Final Accounting
The straight and right path
The complete Book of Deeds
The unified community of believers

n.text: [ARA3] Surah Al-Ikhlas begins with the words Qul huwa Allahu a...

questiontext.text: Surah Al-Ikhlas begins with the words Qul huwa Allahu ahad which mean:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: By the name of Allah the Most Gracious
In the beginning Allah created the heavens and the earth
Say: He is Allah, One and Unique
All praise and thanks belong to Allah Lord of all the worlds

n.text: [ARA3] The phrase Iyyaka na'budu wa iyyaka nasta'in means:...

questiontext.text: The phrase Iyyaka na'budu wa iyyaka nasta'in means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Guide us always to the straight path
In the name of Allah the Most Gracious the Most Merciful
All praise is for Allah Lord of all creation
You alone we worship and You alone we ask for help

n.text: [ARA3] Learning the word-by-word meaning of Surah Al-Fatihah h...

questiontext.text: Learning the word-by-word meaning of Surah Al-Fatihah helps Muslims because:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: It allows faster memorization without needing to understand tajweed rules
It transforms the seventeen daily recitations from automatic habit into conscious encounter with Allah
It completely eliminates the need to learn any further Arabic grammar
It enables finishing the entire Quran more quickly than standard recitation

category.text: $course$/top/Grade 4 / Arabic Language / Unit 4 - Stories and Conversation in Arabic

n.text: [ARA4] Kayfa haluka? means:...

questiontext.text: Kayfa haluka? means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: What is your full name?
Where exactly are you going right now?
How are you doing?
What do you want to do?

n.text: [ARA4] The Arabic past tense verb form dhahaba means:...

questiontext.text: The Arabic past tense verb form dhahaba means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: He currently goes somewhere
He will go in the future
He went somewhere
Please go now

n.text: [ARA4] The Arabic time word fajatan means:...

questiontext.text: The Arabic time word fajatan means:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: At the very end or finally
First of all or to begin with
Therefore or as a result
Suddenly or without warning

n.text: [ARA4] To say I went in the Arabic past tense you say:...

questiontext.text: To say I went in the Arabic past tense you say:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Yadhhabu
Dhahaba
Dhahabtu
Idhhab

n.text: [ARA4] A good Arabic qissa (story) requires:...

questiontext.text: A good Arabic qissa (story) requires:

incorrectfeedback.text: Review the lesson content and try again.

answer.text: Action scenes only with no dialogue between characters
A clear character, setting, problem, and resolution
As many different characters as possible to demonstrate vocabulary range
A completely happy ending with no difficulty or conflict whatsoever
